text: Sharpens a-0021/MG1' using out-of-sample data, but cannot resolve q-0011 (still needs the paid cross-model sweep). e-0018 shows the externalization-saving term — the no-tools agent's realized in-head output, which a-0020 identifies as what LOCATES the Goldilocks peak — is NOT a clean function of formal difficulty: two tier-3 puzzles of near-equal grade (3789 vs 4547 search_nodes) differ 3.66x in none-output (255k vs 70k tok). Therefore MG1' should be restated: the peak of [OUT_saved*P_OUT - CR_added*P_CR] is non-decreasing in model capability when indexed by each model's REALIZED in-head output volume, not by search_nodes. Practical consequence for the e-0010 design: the cross-model peak must be plotted against realized none-output (an observable), and several puzzles per formal tier are needed because grade alone is a 3-4x-noisy proxy for in-head load. Status inconclusive: no cross-model data exists; this is a design-level refinement of the prediction, not its test.
